Thanks!

entry.title: Re: LMS Search for Library-No libraries were found with t...
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your quick respond. The admin role does have the permission and no restrictions on the entities. The admin is able to view the libraries but not able to add item to the library. They have permission to do these task.

entry.title: How to download/extract custom Fiori app source code from ECC
entry.content:
Hi folks, 

I'm trying to download/extract the source code of some custom Fiori apps that have been deployed to our ECC system. These were developed and deployed around 9 years ago and the consultant that developed is long gone. The reason I want to get the project folders is so that I can upload them into BAS if I need to make changes but also have them in case we want to use it for our S4 migration happening soon. I can see the applications in SE80 but there's no to download them from SE80 as a BSP application. Any advice, tips, or suggestion I would very much appreciate. Please ask me anything if you want clarity as well.

entry.title: Re: Update Employee Type in Learning
entry.content: Thank you! I ended up using your suggestion to change the User Source System, which then allowed me to change the Employee Type field by changing Finance settings, then used import data to update the data source back to correct system.

Felipe Cadena

use this report to download ui5 app.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation : coming soon on MacOS !
entry.content:
Hi Experts!, 

I was trying to get my first automation project on my trial btp subaccount, and SAP BPA, I go to the Update Agent and then to the Download Page, but it's not showing the button to download the Agent (image attached). Can anyone help me to understand what is missing in order to download the Agent? Thanks a lot. Best regards.

entry.title: Auto Usage Decision for SAP PM Calibration Process
entry.content:
This blog might be helpful for rare client requirements: Auto Usage Decision for the SAP PM Calibration Process, offering the easiest standard approach. It can also assist implementation consultants in proposing solutions for the design of Calibration Orders' UD (Usage Decision) without human intervention.

 

Client Requirement: Auto Usage Decision for SAP PM Calibration Process

Creation Variant:

  • Step 1: Enter T-code QA40.
  • Step 2: Select the radio button "Orders Released".
  • Step 3: Press CTRL+SAVE, then save the variant with a name and short description.
  • Step 4: Variant is saved with the program RQEVA150.
  • Step 5: Follow the standard Batch Job creation process for the PM Calibration Process.

 

Batch Job Creation Process for Auto Usage in SAP PM:

  • Step 1: Access SM36 (Define Background Job).
  • Step 2: Enter a descriptive Job Name.
  • Step 3: Select Job Class (A, B, or C based on priority).
  • Step 4: Define Job Step by clicking the "Step" button.
  • Step 5: Enter the Program Name (e.g., IPM_ORD_CREATE).
  • Step 6: Define Selection Parameters (e.g., plant, order type).
  • Step 7: Set Job Variants (if applicable).
  • Step 8: Set Date and Time for job execution (or schedule periodically).
  • Step 9: Save the job.
  • Step 10: Use SM37 to monitor job execution and check logs/results.

Best regards,

Sharanabasanagouda.M

For Previous month calculation you need to be select "previous" month period

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Situation type PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR not found
entry.content: @ana_goncalves My situation type is running well. there are some object keys there in monitor instances. but I not get any notification in inbox in Fiori and mail at all. my user name is in Team in recipient. Pls advise

entry.title: Re: SAP Data Services Upgrade to 4.3 SP3 on Azure cloud -...
entry.content:
Hi Kiran,

There are 03 scenario's is up to you which one to pick as per your landscape.

Scenario I: - If network connectivity or network latency is not an issue, then better to move current H/W from on-prem to cloud using Azure Tool in this case only you have to make sure that after migration below configuration are in SYNC

  • O.S H/W configuration (CPU, RAM & Virtual Memory) status before and after migration
  • Drive space status before and after migration
  • Data store connectivity status before and after migration
  • Source file (Network Path) connectivity status before and after migration
  • Assuming machine name is same, so that no impact to current configuration only changes of IP address which will be taken care with th ehelp of network team (firewall security)

Once On-Prem to cloud migration is done, you are done with the post validation take a break for 1-2 weeks and then go for upgrade in this scenario you will not face any issue related to credentials and connectivity.

Scenario II: - Build new environment with same IPS & BODS version, assuming database is pointing to same repositories (CMS, Audit, Local repository, Secure Central repository & Profile repository)

  • Perform installation with dummy database and then switch it to current CMS & Audit database so that you will get the same configuration
  • Activate Local repository, Secure Central repository & Profile repository via CMC console
  • Update Job Server configuration.

Once on-prem IPS & BODS version setup is done, with the post validation take a break for 1-2 weeks and then go for upgrade in this scenario you need ODBC to be setup in new server, also you need credential for all data store where credential is required, also you have to make sure Source file (Network Path) connectivity will work as expected or else coordinate with network team and get it sorted.

Scenario III: - Build new environment with updated IPS & BODS version, using promotion manager copy the content from on-prem landscape.

  • Perform installation with new CMS & Audit database
  • Using Promotion manager migrate the content
  • Create new Local repository, Secure Central repository & Profile repository via DSMC
  • Activate Local repository, Secure Central repository & Profile repository via CMC console
  • Import ATL in new Local repository
  • Delete Job server details from Local repository via DB's.
  • Map new Local repository to Job Server
  • Validate the data store, changes of failing where ever network path and credential is required, please make sure you have credential for all DB's also network connectivity should be in SYNC with new Azure server

I hope this will help!!!

Regards,

Daya Jha

entry.title: Re: Numbering during create and create by association in RAP
entry.content:
Does the use of UUID comes under early numbering concept in manage scenario ? rest is clear to me, they are just normal counter methods if we not use early numbering in behavior definition  we can still leverage this functionality in cbaitem and during creation of header id in unmanage scenario.

My doubt is what are industry standard ?

Because we usually use number range in projects and UUID just throws a random number, what if i want a set range like from 1000 with SNO-1000 sequence in my manage scenario ?

Thanks @Ismail_05 For Blog

entry.title: Re: Inquiry about MRP plan order generation divided into...
entry.content:
Hello Kimminah,

I think you are in a Make-2-Stock scenario using e.g. strategy 40 and lot size EX. Based on this the smallest unit for the MRP is a day. All requirements from a day will get covered by one planned order. The MRP will use the production version with the higher quantity as requirement. In you case it is 50:50 but if it would be 40:60 than the MRP would pick the production version with the 60%-PIR. This is the standard behaviour by the system.

As an alternative: If you just want to make sure that your demands are split between the two existing production versions, you can also work with quota arrangements.

Regards

Johannes

entry.title: Re: Hey, can anyone tell my which scope item describes th...
entry.content:
Hello Jana,

there is only the scope item BLL available, but there are not further details about how to use serial numbers in the production process. 

Here you can find some more details: https://help.sap.com/

Regards

Johannes

entry.title: Re: How to generate PIR with production version?
entry.content:
Hello Kimminah,

unfortunately the maintain PIR app does not support the production version. At the moment, there is also no possibility using the API for S/4HANA Public Cloud. The API also does not support production versions for PIR.

Regards Johannes

entry.title: Re: Requirement to Verify Group Journal Entries
entry.content: Hi Abha, That’s a great question! Group Journal Entry Workflow is an essential feature for financial consolidation, and having a way to track approvals is crucial. While SAP provides Verify General Journal Entries for standard journals, for Group Journal Entries, you might need a custom Fiori report or a workaround using Manage Workflows for Journal Entries.

entry.title: Re: How to Pull Header text of Purchase Requisition to Pu...
entry.content:
Hello,

please check this post: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/copy-purchase-requisition-header-note-to-purchase-order/qaq-p/12369331

Regards

Johannes

entry.title: Re: Purchasing group not auto populating in Purchase requ...
entry.content:
Hello Sandip,

  • Configuration Check: Ensure that the configuration in the MMPUR_V_MG2PGRP table is correct and active. Sometimes, even a small typo can cause issues.
  • User Settings: Verify if there are any user-specific settings that might be overriding the default values. In the "Create Purchase Requisition - Advanced" app, you can check the Personal Settings to see if any default values are set that might be interfering

Regards Johannes

entry.title: Re: connection options between S/4 Hana Private Cloud and...
entry.content:
SAP router is usually used to expose the system to external networks.
You mentioned customer has S/4 Hana Rise means S/4 Hana Private cloud i am not sure what is link between SAP on-premise router and S/4 Hana Rise and how it will help for integration.

What is your requirement exactly then it will be easy to understand?
Usually we will send the payroll posting document from ECP system to S/4 hana for financial posting.
If this is the requirement yes this is possible using IDOCS there is an implementation guide provided by SAP for this.
Refer this link:https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL_PAYROLL/e8a7877791cb4015a062c852fc0340b9/e1fdde1cec9942a695cb764eb00a2b91.html

Let me know if you have further queries.

Hello,

I would suggest to use the Activate method.Use the roadmap viewer and select SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
https://me.sap.com/roadmapviewer

Based on the phase you receive all necessary steps to plan.

Regards Johannes

entry.title: Filtering Worklist in Custom POD
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I would like to use Worklist plugin in CUSTOM POD in SAP Digital Manufacturing.
Since there is no POD Selection plugin available in the CUSTOM POD, how can we fetch worklist items.
Is there a way that we could use 'custom UI Pod Plugin' to filter 'Core worklist Plugin'.

Thanks in Advance!

Anvesh

entry.title: Re: Positioning SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Thank you for your message and for sharing your experience with the new BTP Business Suite learning journey.

If you are unable to enter data in the test environment, this could be due to several reasons, such as missing authorizations, a temporary system issue, or specific configuration requirements for this particular training environment.

Here are a few steps you could try:

  1. Check your assigned role and authorizations: In some cases, learners need to be assigned specific roles to interact with the test system.
  2. Verify browser compatibility: Ensure you are using a supported browser (Chrome, Edge, etc.) and clear cache/cookies if necessary.
  3. Check if the system is in read-only mode: Some training environments are initially set to read-only, and activation for data entry might be triggered by completing a previous step in the learning journey.
  4. Reach out to the official training support: If the issue persists, I would recommend contacting the learning platform’s support team directly — they can check your account setup and test environment configuration.

(This message was drafted with the support of ChatGPT, who helps me sometimes with quick replies and technical explanations.)

entry.title: Cost Calculation in inter Company Sales Process
entry.content:
Inter Company Sales

1. Customer Places order in A Company  with Plant of B company

2. Goods are delivered from B Company to the end customer

3. A company will raise an invoice to the Customer(COGS in B Company)

4. B will raise an inter Company invoice to A Company

Can anyone explain how is cost calculated in both invoices

Is there any process where COGS entry will automatically happen in ordering company after doing PGI in deliver company for intercompany sales process?

entry.title: Unlocking Business Opportunities at Siemens through the Power of Localization and Clean Core
entry.content:
As a global business leader, navigating the complexities of local regulations and market specificities while maintaining a large-scale operation can be daunting. At SAP, we have seen the transformative power of localization — the strategic adaptation of business processes to meet local needs. Today, I want to share how Siemens is leveraging SAP's localization capabilities to streamline its global operations. We had the pleasure of speaking with two seasoned professionals at Siemens: Ms. Andrea Spandau and Mr. Uwe Stuerzenhofecker, experienced transformation leaders in the area of Tax and Customs processes. Their insights provide a deeper understanding of how the company is navigating this transformative process and the critical role of localization in ensuring global success.

Driving Enterprise Success - The Siemens Journey

Siemens serves various sectors, including industry, infrastructure, mobility, and healthcare. As a global powerhouse operating in more than 170 countries and boasting a rich history of innovation and reliability over its 175-year journey, Siemens has consistently adapted to the shifting business landscapes of digitalization and sustainability. The implementation of a clean core ERP system has been a game-changer. By aligning business processes with strategy and maintaining global standards, Siemens ensures lean operations and enhanced focus on delivering customer-centric solutions. This approach not only streamlines internal processes but significantly boosts customer satisfaction as clients experience tailored services that genuinely meet their local needs.

A key aspect of Siemens' transformation is the SHERPA X program, which aims to migrate the company's ERP systems to SAP S/4HANA. This ambitious project involves more than 1,000 legal entities across 60 different ERP systems, with SAP being the primary platform.

The Strategic Role of Harmonization and Localization

Localization is an integral part of the Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program. Siemens recognizes that a global template does not fit every country, and local legal requirements must be met. The localization process starts with deciding which country will be localized, followed by the creation of a country-specific blueprint that serves as a basis for future businesses operating in that country.

Siemens has a dedicated localization team that collaborates with regional experts to conduct fit-gap analyses and ensure that the template covers all legal requirements. This process involves a series of workshops and deep-dive sessions, engaging local experts to virtually discuss local legal requirements to be implemented for the end-to-end processes such as lead to cash, purchase to pay and record to report.

Siemens faces the challenge of aligning its vast operations across different regulatory, tax, and business landscapes. The solution? A robust localization strategy encompassed within their Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program. Here’s how they do it:

  1. Developing Country-Specific Blueprint: Starting with a baseline blueprint that encompasses all legal requirements of a country, Siemens crafts a foundational layer essential for compliance. This strategic move allows the company to confidently and swiftly enter new markets.
  2. Incorporating Business-Specific Adaptations: On top of this compliance layer, Siemens integrates specific business processes that are crucial for meeting the localized demands. This dual approach ensures that while the global standards are met, the solutions resonate well with local customers. Harmonization, in Siemens’ strategy, means standardizing core processes across the company while ensuring flexibility. The Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program focuses on creating a lean ERP core where indispensable functionalities reside, simplifying maintenance and integration of new technologies or processes.
  3. Ensuring Integrity and Consistency: A key aspect of Siemens’ strategy includes employing SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) to uphold data integrity across all their ERP systems. With MDG, Siemens streamlines data modifications which are only permissible in the central system, thereby maintaining consistency across all customer transactions and financial processes. This strategic governance assures that regardless of geographical boundaries, every operation within Siemens aligns with the core business standards.

A Beacon for Global Enterprises

One of the guiding principles of Siemens' localization approach is maintaining a lean ERP core. Siemens’ commitment to a clean core and comprehensive localization not only benefits their operation but also empowers their clients.

Siemens employs a range of SAP solutions, such as SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) for e-invoicing, SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) for customs and export control, and SAP Business Integrity Screening. These solutions work in harmony with the ERP system, providing a comprehensive and efficient platform for global operations.

The journey Siemens is on serves as an inspiring blueprint for many other global entities. By sharing their strategies and successes, we aim to embolden more enterprises to embrace and leverage localization in their business transformation endeavors. As we continue partnering with companies across various industries and regions, SAP remains dedicated to offering the essential tools and insights required for mastering the intricacies of global operations.

Summary

Siemens' journey towards digitalization and standardization is a testament to the company's commitment to staying ahead in an ever-evolving business landscape. By leveraging SAP's localization capabilities and maintaining a clean core, Siemens is well-positioned to navigate the complexities of global operations and deliver value to its customers.

As the Tax & Customs team continues to drive the specific localization requirements within the Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program, they are not only transforming Siemens' internal processes but also setting an example for other global enterprises looking to streamline their operations. The strategies and practices developed during this journey will undoubtedly serve as a valuable guide for other organizations aiming for global success. Through innovative localization and a focus on clean core principles, Siemens is paving the way for a more efficient, compliant, and customer-centric future.

Learn about Localization Today 

Siemens: How Can Automated Document Processing Reduce Compliance Costs Across the Globe?

Localization Capabilities on sap.com 

Accelerate Global Business Readiness with Localized SAP S/4HANA - SAPinsider

SAP Community for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

SAP Community for Localization 

Regulatory Change Manager: Your Solution to Navigate Regulatory Changes with Ease

entry.title: Warning message from BADI SD_SLS_CHECK_ITEM not shown in VA02 - Shown in fiori app Manage Sales Ord
entry.content:
Hello all,

 

We have an issue, a custom warning message that is being raised in the implementation of BADI SD_SLS_CHECK_ITEM (Key user extenisibility) is triggered both in the VA02 transaction and in the fiori apps Manages Sales Order BUT only in the fiori apps can we see the warning message being displayed at save.

I've checked for both triggers (VA02 - Manage Sales Orders), in both cases the parameter "messages" is populated with the custom message. It's unclear to me why the message isn't shown for the VA02 flow. 

Has anyone experienced a similar issue in the past? How did you solve it.

 

NOTE: We trigger the BADI implementation successfully in both cases.

entry.title: Re: Public Sector Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402
entry.content:
Hi Mary,

Thank you very much for sharing  article. Very interesting topic. Can you please point out to a link of BPML process of L1 L2 L3 and L4 for Public Cloud scope items. Also assist me to know for any customer should we have to go by L-1 or latest support pack in Public Cloud Implememtations.

Regards,

C R S

6YU

Government Budget Management

71S

Grants Mgt

7AU

Fund Accounting

78S

Earmarked Funds

6UC

Public Sector Budget Planning

6R3

Earmarked Funds Generation from CLM

6X0

Earmarked Funds for Position Budget Control

7O6

US GASB

7QE

Account Mapping for US GASB

Grants Mgt

Earmarked Funds

6UC

Public Sector Budget Planning

6R3

6X0

Earmarked Funds for Position Budget Control

7O6

US GASB

7QE

Account Mapping for US GASB

Regards,

C.R.Srinath

entry.title: Accessing BTP backend Application using the Authentication provided by SAP CIS
entry.content:
Dear All, I am at a confusing spot while choosing services for my usecase.

Usecase: there exists a simple custom NodeJS backend service in a subaccount bound to a XSUAA instance

(For now lets limit our discussion to only client like POSTMAN or curl accessing the the backend)

Global Account and the Subaccount are trusted using the Default Identity of SAP (I am using the trial account to do this POC before i got to my customers SAP BTP)

When i use the OAuth endpoints of the XSUAA instance to simulate a OAuth Code grant flow, i am able to successfully get the access_token and others. Using this access token i am able to hit my backend and get the response. Any attempt to malform the token leads to 401 Unauthorized which is perfect!

Now comes the question: Assume I add a Cloud identity Service Subscription to my Global Account and then establish the trust in my subaccount. Then do the Role Collection Mapping with the Groups that i created in the CIS for various users. Now the same OAuth flow can be done using the Application created in the CIS and i get a token. What do i need to do differently that the above steps to let XSUAA trust the access_token that i get from CIS? At the moment i am getting 401 Unauthorized, rightfully so, because i dont see any scopes or roles getting attached to the access_token and hence naturally the XSUAA will reject it.

Any hints will be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Tips for renewing SSL Certs on the Web Dispatcher
entry.content:
There is no need to restart the webdispacher.

Just go to the webdisppatcher admin page, and initiate a Reload of the pse.

Goto: WebDispachter => Core System => Active Services
Click "Change Service"
do not change anything
Click "Change Service" to save, this will also reload the PSE.

entry.title: Re: can SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition be set to be Sin...
entry.content: Please check this link https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/sso-in-s-4hana-cloud-public/qaq-p/12812023

I am a student and I want to be do the practices at home in the SAP FICO & SD Module but I have found same error from server provider it was charged too high cost for the practices and server is very slow, so today I am finally think that I should practices in offline server, hence I requesting to all sap user & expert please suggest a batter way for the same.

Please guide me how to install SAP offline in my PC please share any link, video or full setup for the above mention query.

Hope you all will give me a great solution for the same

Regards,

Sonam Singh

entry.title: Re: CDS Views for Price Simulation of Customer/Material C...
entry.content:
There is no CDS for Your requirements.

Please try API

https://api.sap.com/api/MaintenanceLookupTableDataService/overview

entry.title: Re: Error Message - Server is busy
entry.content:
That can happen when the printer is "busy" processing multiple print jobs. Often see this with older printer drivers.

What printer are they using?

how many reports is the user trying to print?

are they large report, multiple pages or single pages etc.?

Do you have any logging built into your app to get details?

entry.title: Blog series on my CPI + Camel learning journey
entry.content:
Intro / Motivation

Disclaimer: This series is written by a person unrelated to camel or even java world beforehand. Please, do NOT expect proficiency in EAI, EIP, OSGI or even Spring basics. There is a much better  way of testing proper camel routes natively. And most likely cap java would have been a better stack for the tool I am presenting.

So, finally I got involved in some project where I would touch CPI (or Integration Suite as they call it now).

And as a not at all fan of low-code tools I instantly felt not at all at home there (who would have thought).

Cuz as a developer I am used to having all my artifacts editable in convenient IDE and then stored in VCS (to track changes properly), to being able to run the stuff locally in order to debug and test it, and also to automate the latter becuase I am rather lazy person (but we call it Quality to trick clients into paying for that).

And as you might know, this is not something you have in CPI out of the box (wysiwyg iflow editor without undo feature? yeah, okay...).

Luckily though, there are some apis (public and not so much), with help of which you can build tools yourself.

And I was actually rather surprised not to discover those tools as some open-source projects, so I decided to try creating one for myself using sap cap (and some vanilla nodejs stuff).

So, this blog series is my take on working with cpi locally and learning camel (but avoiding writing Java code) in the meantime.

 

How we typically test stuff

As a quick reminder though, it makes sense to point out that in order to be able to test something, you need to start writing testable code.

And in case of CPI it means some iflow steps need to be modelled differently - as external rather than embedded into iflow, like script steps or message mappings - because then osgi magic would allow us to run bundles and connect them (locally we only have blueprint deployed).

And then only thing we need is to be able to isolate and run our stuff.

Preferably on local machine.

This figure above is taken from nice open sap course on abap unit testing to sum it all up

Btw, course was not migrated after they killed it, but who needs testing anyway..

 

Series structure or plans

Currently I have at least these 3 parts to follow up in mind (in March I guess) with approximate agendas:

  • About the tool from the technical perspective and Intended workflow (mention other tools for groovy scripting/xslt) + project setup + git
  • Camel / karaf / osgi basics + comparing cpi flows from guidelines to camel routes and playing with them
  • More stuff about approach to testing (manual and automated) and isolation (pollenrich vs external systems etc)

 

Demo time

And finally, there's the teaser.

Thanks for reading and stay tuned!

00:00 pt1: Initial setup and remote webshell

06:06 pt2: Karaf setup and local webshell

09:46 pt3.1: Deploy and prepare sample flows from guidelines (cpi + local)

13:27 pt3.2: Manual testing of sample flows with ui

19:23 pt3.3 Running automated test for a flow

21:38 Outro (git sync feature was not mentioned, but will be covered in next posts)

entry.title: Unable to download Desktop Agent for SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am currently working on a DOX (Document information extraction) Bot project in SAP Build Process Automation; and I am following the tutorial (sap-tutorials/sap-build-process-automation) about "Install and Set Up Desktop Agent 3" from @pbujnicka. I followed the documentation to create the user in RBSC portal, having an S-number user in SAP BTP trial cockpit, and set up the Secret ID.

But when I go to the Control Tower, and then to the Download Page, the box with the droplist to download the Agent installation file does not appear (image attached). Note: The role ProcessAutomationAdmin is already assigned to the user, and the technical user is created, and Secret ID generated and in Status: "Valid".

There is no error message and nothing to know what is going wrong.

@peterengel @SAPSupport @pbujnicka 

Please let me know, if I am missing out on anything or what is the cause of this problem and of course how can I fix it.

Thank you in advance,

Best Regards,

Rosa.

entry.title: Re: 在启用高级评估之后，计提坏账准备时，需要勾选客户主数据中的信用参数文件的风险类，导致必须启用信用额度；不勾...
entry.content: 谢谢指导

entry.title: 在执行付款策略后，形成清算的银行科目后，后续如果过过账到总账对账单中，或者说导入对账项目后，如何形成冲销清算科目
entry.content: # SAP S/4HANA Cloud# SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public# SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition# SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance,#，1.在执行付款策略后，形成清算的银行科目后即借：应付账款 贷：银行清算科目11001020，后续如果过过账到总账对账单中，或者说导入对账项目后，如何形成冲销清算科目即将11001000月11001020的冲销，导入对账单后的过账未能自动冲销银行清算科目；2.相关实施未执行J78，能否实现上述基本功能；3.是否有先关Scope-item银行管理和对账单相关的，

entry.title: Re: Change Rate Italy Bank
entry.content:
You can create a RFC in SM59

define the SSL certificate in security options section 

Download the certificate from https://tassidicambio.bancaditalia.it/

Import the certificate in strust

Test RFC conection and you will see all exchange rates from banca de italia

You can base on the standar program RFIMPNBS to copy it and upload the exchgange rates from this source.

Share if you reach it.

Regards,

Gerardo

entry.title: Re: 银行清算科目如何结转至银行主科目?
entry.content: 麻烦问下，能否帮忙更新下这个配置的链接，过去没有找到相关配置及操作

entry.title: Re: I created the SAP GUI AI Agent
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for your patience. I'm currently recording an episode where I dive into some architecture and code. I expect to wrap it up by tomorrow, so it should be published early next week. Apart from the video, I will also try to write a few words about it.

BR

entry.title: Re: Unable to download WSDL file from a webservice
entry.content:
Hello Shiva,

I am new to SAP, and after many years of your issue, I got the same one. 
What I did to solve the error was to deploy the business configuration. But that didn't worked out and got another error saying the solution is locked. 
So I activated all the bo and ui components in the solution and tried deploying again. Even this time, I got the same error saying the solution is locked. But, when I tried to download the file, I was successful. 

Hope this helps someone.

Regards,

Nag.

The example on the Accelerator Hub does not have a Body.

SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Many thanks 🙂

entry.title: 'Key "ID" is missing for entity "ProcessorService.Incidents"' error
entry.content:
"I was following the 'Enable draft with @odata.draft.enabled' section of https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/add-fiori-elements-uis.html, and after adding 'annotate ProcessorService.Incidents with @odata.draft.enabled;' to the srv/services.cds file, I'm getting an error in the browser saying 'Key "ID" is missing for entity "ProcessorService.Incidents"'. Does anyone know what the problem is?

The Incidents entity is currently as follows (not modified from the manual):

 

entity Incidents : cuid, managed {  
customer     : Association to Customers;
title        : String  @title : 'Title';
urgency        : Association to Urgency default 'M';
status         : Association to Status default 'N';
conversation  : Composition of many {
    key ID    : UUID;
    timestamp : type of managed:createdAt;
    author    : type of managed:createdBy;
    message   : String;
};
}

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Management Layer for SAP S/4HANA – What’s New – Release 2023
entry.content:
Hi Petra,

currently we are having S4HANA 2022 system with EML version 2022.

I have few question on EML version upgrade point of view .
Can we upgrade/Update the EML version to EML 2023  without upgrading the whole system(S4HANA)?
If yes then How can we achieve this? 
If No and S4HANA upgrade is the only option to have EML 2023 , then what need to be done/Taken care after the upgrade? as my understanding is upgrade would “only” provide the functionality, not the configuration.

Could you please advice as we are currently doing for future roadmap planning .

entry.title: Re: When I move to Quality system from Dev my BW Query OD...
entry.content: The manual of Sandra_Rosi is one of the ways. Easier is (re)using FM RSEQ_NAT_GENERATION. There you place the query technical name, tr package and tr request, not the transport itself. The it collects all the necessary data.

entry.title: How to use value help when a field has over 200 statuses?
entry.content: I have a requirement to setup a report that needs a value help. however, this status field (ekbe-bwart) does not have an associated cds view and has over 200 options. What would be the best way to go about doing this?

entry.title: Re: Fiori & CAP: getting custom action working
entry.content:
hi Sandeep,

thanks for the short explanation. I have already read several times the page you have linked to your answer. This page is describing the situation of having a table and one or many rows are selected before a bound action is working. In this case, the context is delivered to the backend service call related to my action.

In my case, I like to have an action using the annotation-based approach with an input parameter to enter the title and bound to the entity which the complete object page is bound to. The term "bound" means in my case not the table which itself is completely bound to the object page as well. This case is not described in the Fiori docu. 

I was able to setup an unbound action with annotations, the action button will be displayed in the table toolbar. The expected popup dialog is shown if I press the action button and I can enter the title. After confirming the dialog close button, a CREATE request with the CAP action. But the CAP backend service is getting no context (I mean the bound entity) due to the fact that unbound actions never get a context as described in the Fiori docu.

My simple question is: is it possible to get the context (the bound entity) or alternatively, to have an input parameter containing at least the GUID from the underlying bound object page's entity  into the backend call so the CAP backend can use this GUID to select the context itself? Is this possible using the annotations? Or do I have to use the custom extensions in combination with manifest.json and do I have to write the complete dialog popup etc. on my own?

The only answer I know if I like to use the annotation based approach is to use global actions in the object page header. But I like to display the action button on the table toolbar insetad because the action button is for manipulations of all rows of this table and not of the entity which is bound to the complete object page.

 

Yours

Stephan

entry.title: Re: Call an OData Web API Service externally
entry.content:
Hi @junwu 

If by chance, you know. 

I'm trying to create a Destination in SAP BTP (to consume it in SAP BAS) , with the new address  without -web, Authentication is OAuth2ClientCredentials. I provide Token service URL, service id and client secret. But unfortunately, getting a 401 error.

entry.title: Re: Issue with address inconsistencies when synchronizing...
entry.content: Hi Marco, yes I found my issue! There were 2 entries for the same Pers. number in table PA0000. The first one didn't have end or start date, and with those fields blank, it gave me the error. We deleted that entry so it could use the second one with the correct data (and with the proper values in start and end date

entry.title: Re: How do we call SOLR externally with SSO enabled?

entry.title: Re: ABAP CERTIFICATION
entry.content:
Certification for ABAP  Cloud is here https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-back-end-developer-abap-cloud#how-to-prepare

You can learn from : 

  • https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/acquire-core-abap-skills
  • https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/practicing-clean-core-extensibility-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud

Additionally You can learn from books which You can buy from SAP PRESS

  • https://www.sap-press.com/

Example test You can perform on this platform: 

  • https://www.erpprep.com/sap-cloud-solutions/c-abapd-2309-sap-abap-cloud-back-end-developer
  • https://www.udemy.com/courses/search/?src=ukw&q=C_ABAPD

entry.title: Re: Country of origin in invoice, pro-forma , etc
entry.content: Information for sales document type are determined by material master data, business partner data or from customer info record. Country of origin from purchasing info record is not taken to sales document types.

entry.title: Re: How to use value help when a field has over 200 statu...
entry.content:
@junwuthe cds code 

 

define view ZMSTATUS_VH as select distinct from ekbe as mt
{
  key mt.bwart as mstatus
}
group by mt.bwart

 

Displays as such:

 As you can see, not all the statuses are showing up as the values in the SAP system are such:

entry.title: How to access Purchase Requisition Personal Setting (ME52N) from custom dialog
entry.content:
I'm trying to display the dialog to maintain Purchase Requisition Personal Settings from a custom program, but only the tab which is about the default values.

Is it possible?

entry.title: Re: How to access Purchase Requisition Personal Setting (...
entry.content:
Below is a little snippet to obtain this dialog:

It's mostly derived from MEGUI_MAINTAIN function module (based on CL_FRAMEWORK_MM, CL_PERSONALIZATION_MM, CL_PROPOSER_MM, CL_ESDU_CONNECTOR_MM_2, etc.) Instead of calling the function module MEPERS_START_POPUP which was too much tricky, I used MEPERS_START_ITEM_PROP to start the popup.

REPORT.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN 1001.
  PARAMETERS dummy.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF SCREEN 1001.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  cl_framework_mm=>get_instance( IMPORTING ex_instance = DATA(framework) ).

  " Class CL_PERSONALIZATION_MM
  DATA(personalization) = framework->get_personalization( ).

  " For the object type if_purchase_requisition=>c_objtyp = 'BUS2105' (Purchase Requisition), the
  " proposer class will be FUNCTION-POOL=MEPROP\CLASS=LCL_REQ_PROPOSER. You MUST indicate the
  " DDIC structure MEREQ_PROP which is hardcoded in LCL_REQ_PROPOSER.
  CONSTANTS po_doc TYPE mepo_mfs_application VALUE 'MMPUR_PO_DOC'. " From the constants in LMEGUICON
  DATA(l_proposer) = cl_proposer_mm=>create( im_objecttype      = EXACT #( if_purchase_requisition=>c_objtyp )
                                             im_structure       = 'MEREQ_PROP'           ##NO_TEXT
                                             im_mfs_application = po_doc
                                             im_postfix         = '_X' ).

  framework->add_proposer( im_objecttype = EXACT #( if_purchase_requisition=>c_objtyp )
                           im_proposer   = l_proposer ).
  personalization->append( im_name   = 'ReqProposer'          ##NO_TEXT
                           im_object = l_proposer ).
  DATA(esdus_stream) = NEW cl_esdus_connector_mm_2( im_action_id = 'PurchaseRequisition' ) ##NO_TEXT. " ESDUS-ACTION
  esdus_stream->if_reader_mm~read_root( personalization ).

  " Function module 'MEPERS_START_ITEM_PROP' must be called inside a screen sequence because
  " in l_popup->send() (CL_WINDOW_MM), there is "PERFORM set_screen IN PROGRAM (prog) USING old_dyn"
  " (PROG = '') which does a SET SCREEN and later, the end of program produces a short dump DYNPRO_NOT_FOUND.
  CALL SELECTION-SCREEN 1001.

  ASSERT 1 = 1.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  IF sy-dynnr = '1001'.
    DATA(l_save) = EXACT mmpur_bool( abap_false ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'MEPERS_START_ITEM_PROP'
      EXPORTING im_objecttype = EXACT string40( if_purchase_requisition=>c_objtyp )
      IMPORTING ex_savesetup  = l_save.
    IF l_save = abap_true.
      " Transfer screen input to global data in function group of ES_SAVE_USER_SETTINGS.
      " This doesn't transfer "deletions" of 'PurchaseRequisition' personal settings which are not displayed.
      " Don't use ESDUS_STREAM->HANDLE_SAVE_REQUEST( ), which would delete them.
      esdus_stream->if_writer_mm~write_root( personalization ).
      CALL FUNCTION 'ES_SAVE_USER_SETTINGS'.
      COMMIT WORK.
    ENDIF.

    SUPPRESS DIALOG.
    LEAVE TO SCREEN 0.
  ENDIF.

NB: the saved data goes to the table ESDUS.

entry.content: Interesting Read, would definitely come back to this soon.

entry.title: Re: End routine logic not working with multiple record lo...
entry.content: You can easily debug and find the underlying reason. Seems with multiple record conditions are failing. I advise to run the DTP with multiple record and debug the code. You can set the watch point with desired variable value and then debug. I am sure this will make you understand where the code is failing.

entry.title: Re: SAP Standard Datasource Enhancement code select times.
entry.content: in RSA3, you can see the package size and package count. So Open cursor and corresponding select is executed one time for each package count.

entry.title: Re: BW Web Report Is not working in EDGE.
entry.content: Can you please check in RSRT, if you can execute the query in Java web or not?

entry.title: Using SAC Rest API Patch /api/v1/permissions - Has anyone been able to use it?
entry.content:
Hello,

According to SAP Business Accelerator Hub, we should be able to use this API to mass-change object access.

However, the documentation is not adequately defined and there is no example of a body for the PATCH request.

Has anyone been able to find the correct specification?

Even if you "Try out" SAP forgot to update the API.

SAP Analytics Cloud API

This functionality can only be genuinely leveraged for brand new developments.

I hope SAP provides a way to solve this.

entry.title: Re: Cascading picklist for Region of Birth
entry.content: How was this issue resolved ?

entry.title: Central Store-Based Provisioning: The Ultimate Solution for Application User Sync from IPS 🔁
entry.content:
Central store-based provisioning enables the automatic provisioning of application-specific groups from the Identity Directory to the target systems whenever changes occur. These changes include user assignments or modifications of group attributes.

Note

Currently, application-specific groups are supported for 

  • SAP Advanced Financial Closing
  • SAP Ariba Applications
  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP Application Server ABAP
  • SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  • SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud
  • Microsoft Entra ID
  • Local Identity Directory provisioning systems

Create Application-Specific Groups or Assign Application-Specific Groups to existing Group

you can create application-specific groups in the Identity Directory of your SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant and provision them afterward to target systems of your choice.

Operating with application-specific groups by Identity Provisioning service requires having a source system with set property ips.application.id. By running provisioning jobs from such source system you can create, update, and delete application-specific groups in the Identity Directory of your SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant, depending on the values of their attributes.

Enable or Disable Central Store-Based Provisioning

You can enable or disable the Central Store-Based Provisioning option in the administration console for SAP Cloud Identity Services.

  • Under Applications and Resources, choose the Applications tile.
  • Choose the application that you want
  • Under the Provisioning tab, enable or disable the Central Store-Based Provisioning option
  • Once the application has been updated, the system displays the message Application <name of application> updated.

When you enable the Central Store-Based Provisioning for a specific application, whenever you update an application-specific group associated with this application, a provisioning of the updates is triggered, there is no need to run manual or scheduled jobs in the Identity Provisioning

IPS Transformation Changes

Select the relevant source system, open the Properties tab, choose Edit and add the property ips.application.id =6f187cce-2f51-4efd-9bf4-9a8aabdd1c9cMonitor Central Store Logs

Central Store Logs provide information about the application-specific groups that have been provisioned from the Identity Directory to your target systems.

Note

Central Store Logs are enabled and will appear under Provisioning Logs whenever changes to application-specific groups occur in the Identity Directory, such as assigning users or modifying group attributes.

 select your Identity Provisioning --->  Provisioning Logs --->  Central Store Logs

entry.title: Re: Split Settlement documents based on one Condition Con...
entry.content: hi victor, I would need your guidance for condition contract solution

entry.title: Re: Split Settlement documents based on one Condition Con...
entry.content: During partial settlement run, customer should get accrual amount as partial payment even if target is not achieved and it should get reversed when Final settlement is executed
